title: dampiera haematotricha subsp. haematotricha
intro: Dampiera haematotricha subsp. haematotricha, a plant species native to Western Australia, is a type of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat wounds and skin irritations due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, Dampiera haematotricha subsp. haematotricha is prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions or extracts, which can be applied topically to the affected area to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
